stem: A factory produces steel beams using two sources. Mill A handles 69% of production with a known defect rate of 2.7%. Mill B handles 31% of production, but its defect rate is uncertain — recent quality audits suggest it lies between 5.9% and 11.3%, depending on raw material batch quality. A randomly selected steel beam is found to be defective. What is the probability it came from Mill B?

ground_truth.answer: P(Mill B | defective) ranges from 0.495 to 0.653 as Mill B's defect rate varies from 5.9% to 11.3%. Point estimate at 8.6%: 0.589.

solution_steps: Apply Bayes' theorem: P(B|def) = P(def|B)×P(B) / [P(def|A)×P(A) + P(def|B)×P(B)].
The interval [0.495, 0.653] captures the range of posterior probabilities.
common_failure_modes: Using only the midpoint defect rate and providing a single answer
Comparing defect rates directly without applying Bayes' theorem
